original_reasoning: Arabic is about farting, an offensive bodily emission, while Greek means insults, an offensive verbal emission; bodily offense and verbal offense form a plausible drift chain.
If Arabic أفوغ points to a mouth-related defect or openness, that plausibly meets Greek excessive eating through the mouth/eating core.

الشمظ=يشمظ الإنسان بكلام / تحريك دون العنف (speaking with gentle words / movement without violence). ἵλασμα=propitiation, atonement. Gentle speech aimed at soothing IS a form of propitiation. The appeasing-through-mild-words concept links them.
Arabic 'worthless fragment/cast-off' and Greek diminutive both preserve smallness/reduction.
Stone dropped to gauge depth points toward bottom or base, plausibly near root.

الإتاوة=الخراج (tribute, tax imposed). Εἵλωτες=Helots (Spartan serfs who paid forced dues). Both relate to forced extraction of wealth/produce from subordinates. The tribute/subjection concept connects them, though one is the payment and the other the people.
Greek 'breasts/chest' shares the bodily-erotic domain with the Arabic intercourse sense.
Arabic play with it or poke at the ground preserves a manipulative or eroticized play notion that can plausibly drift toward the Greek sexual act.
